objective: Recognize that rectangles with the same perimeter can have different areas, and find which has the greatest area

learn.title: 같은 둘레라도 넓이가 다를 수 있다
learn.content: Rectangles with the SAME perimeter can have DIFFERENT areas. Example — all have perimeter 12 cm: 5×1 → Area=5. 4×2 → Area=8. 3×3 → Area=9. The shape closest to a square has the GREATEST area! 예: Three rectangles all have perimeter 16 cm: 7×1, 5×3, 4×4. Which has the greatest area? → 4×4 with area 16 sq cm. (7×1→7, 5×3→15, 4×4→16. The square shape (4×4) has the greatest area.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 넓이 비교 — 정사각형에 가까울수록 큼
learn.content: To compare rectangles with the same perimeter, find the area of each. Area = length × width. The rectangle closest to a square always wins. Example: Perimeter = 20. Options: 9×1→9, 7×3→21, 6×4→24, 5×5→25. Winner: 5×5! 예: Perimeter = 24 cm. Compare: 11×1, 8×4, 6×6. Which has the greatest area? → 6×6 with area 36 sq cm. (11×1=11, 8×4=32, 6×6=36. Square wins!)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 모든 정수 쌍 나열 — l+w = P÷2
learn.content: To list all whole-number rectangles with a given perimeter, use l + w = P ÷ 2, then try all pairs. For P=10: l+w=5. Pairs: (4,1), (3,2). Areas: 4 and 6. The (3,2) rectangle has the greatest area. 예: List all whole-number rectangle pairs with perimeter 14 cm. Which has the greatest area? → Pairs: (6,1)→6, (5,2)→10, (4,3)→12. Greatest area: 4×3 = 12 sq cm.. (l+w = 7. Pairs: (6,1), (5,2), (4,3). Areas: 6, 10, 12.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 정사각형이 최대 넓이 — 핵심 원리
learn.content: 주어진 둘레에서 가장 큰 넓이를 가지는 직사각형은 정사각형. 예: P=20 → 5×5(=25)가 최대. 9×1(=9), 7×3(=21), 6×4(=24)보다 큼. 🔍 왜? 길이와 너비가 균형 잡힐수록 넓이 ↑. 한쪽이 길어지면 다른 쪽이 줄어 곱 ↓.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 가늘고 긴 모양 = 최소 넓이
learn.content: 같은 둘레에서 가장 작은 넓이는 1×(P/2−1) 모양 (가장 가늘고 긴 직사각형). 예: P=20 → 9×1=9 (최소). 🔍 정사각형 25와 비교하면 거의 1/3. 모양이 균형 잃을수록 넓이 손실 큼.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 실생활 최적화 — 정원/축사
learn.content: 농부가 펜스 24 m로 정원을 만든다. 가장 넓은 모양은?   l+w=12 → 쌍: (11,1)=11, (9,3)=27, (7,5)=35, (6,6)=36. 정답: 6×6 정사각형, 넓이 36 m². 🔍 같은 펜스(둘레)로 면적을 최대화 → 정사각형.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 직관 — 모눈종이로 시각화
learn.content: P=12로 만들 수 있는 직사각형 종류:   · 5×1 (얇고 긴 띠 모양) — 면적 5   · 4×2 (직사각형) — 면적 8   · 3×3 (정사각형) — 면적 9 🖍️ 모눈에 그려보면: 길쭉할수록 안쪽 칸 적음, 정사각에 가까울수록 안쪽 풍성.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Which statement is always true for rectangles with the same perimeter?
choices: The longest rectangle has the greatest area
The rectangle closest to a square has the greatest area
They all have the same area
try.answer: The rectangle closest to a square has the greatest area
try.hint: Think about what shape has equal sides.
guided_steps: Step 1: A square has all sides equal — most compact shape.
Step 2: More compact = greater area for same perimeter.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r2.question: Maya has 24 feet of fence to make a rectangular pen for her rabbit. She wants the pen to have the greatest possible area. What dimensions should she use?
practice_r2.answer: 6×6 = 36 sq ft. A square (6×6) gives the greatest area with perimeter 24 ft.

practice_r2.question: A farmer uses 30 m of fence for a rectangular field. He makes it 12 m × 3 m. Could he get more area with the same fence? What dimensions would give more area?
practice_r2.answer: Yes. 12×3=36 sq m. A 7×8 rectangle gives 56 sq m. Even better: 8×7 (same). Closest to square with P=30 is 7×8 or 8×7.

practice_r3.question: Why does the square always have the greatest area among rectangles with the same perimeter? Explain using an example.
practice_r3.answer: The square has sides as equal as possible, making it most compact. Example P=16: 7×1=7, 6×2=12, 5×3=15, 4×4=16. As sides get closer to equal, area increases.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 19 — Same Perimeter, Different Areas
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: A rectangle has perimeter 26 cm and area 40 sq cm. What are the dimensions? Show your work.
practice_r3.answer: l+w=13. Try pairs: 8×5=40 ✓. Length=8 cm, Width=5 cm. Check: (8+5)×2=26 ✓, 8×5=40 ✓.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.5 'Same Perimeter, Different Areas' pp.429-432
